sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain in the hand, rated as a seven out of ten, following a skiing injury. He describes the pain as sharp and localized to the index finger. He also reports chronic constipation and takes miralax for it.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The x-ray of the right index finger reveals no fractures or dislocations, indicating a sprain of the distal interphalangeal joint. Physical examination reveals tenderness to palpation over the DIP joint of the right index finger.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a sprain of the distal interphalangeal joint of the right index finger.
medical_concepts: sprain of the distal interphalangeal joint

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The doctor will put a splint on the right finger. The doctor will prescribe 14 milligrams of mobic once daily. The doctor wants the patient to return in two weeks for follow-up.

medical_concepts: sprain of the distal interphalangeal joint
